body.title: Pre-flight classify-and-heal — sort fixable test rot, repair it, proceed
body.result: preflight_heal module: Recognizer ABC + SocketRecvNoMockRecognizer (matches socket.recv/ReadTimeout → adds @pytest.mark.skipif gated on IGOR_LIVE_TESTS). classify/apply_heal/commit_heal/heal_and_commit full flow + _ensure_imports helper. pe_chain pe_test stores full test_output; run_pe_entry_chain pre-flight branch wires to heal_and_commit with one retry + clean fall-through. 17 new tests pass (4 match + 4 remedy + 2 classify + 2 apply + 3 ensure_imports + 2 full-flow). Commit 4e8cc032.
body.description:
Akien's framing (2026-04-22 post-20:00): 'if preflight can sort if we should fix it anyway while we're there that would be the biggest win.'

Current pe_chain behavior: pre-flight runs pytest in subprocess; on any failure, escalates with reason 'pre-flight: test suite already broken' and parks the ticket. Igor has been stuck in this loop since 20:00 on T-cloud-fallback-alert — SITUATE fires correctly (post the kernel.py fix), pre-flight fails on a socket.recv timeout in some test that makes a live network call, pe_chain escalates, 5 min later the cycle repeats. Zero commits, zero progress.

New behavior: between 'pre-flight failed' and 'escalate,' insert a failure-shape classifier. Three buckets:

1. my-work-caused — test was green on HEAD, red after Igor's edit. Escalate unchanged.
2. already-broken-fixable — failure matches a recognizer pattern with a known remedy. Apply the remedy (edit test file), commit as precursor, re-run pre-flight. If green, proceed with original ticket.
3. already-broken-unfixable — escalate, BUT include classifier output in the reason so future work knows why it was unfixable.

This turns 'already broken' from a dead-end into a self-healing opportunity. The recognizer library grows over time — every new pattern Igor hits becomes a new recognizer.

MVP (this ticket):
- New module wild_igor/igor/tools/preflight_heal.py: Recognizer ABC with matches(failure_text) and remedy(failure_text, repo_root) → list[EditDict]; classify_and_heal(pytest_output, repo_root) → HealResult(healed, edits, unfixable).
- One concrete recognizer: SocketRecvNoMockRecognizer — matches 'socket.timeout: ReadTimeout' or 'return self._sock.recv' patterns from urllib3. Remedy: locate failing test function, add '@pytest.mark.skipif(not os.getenv("IGOR_LIVE_TESTS"), reason="requires live network")'.
- Wire into pe_chain.py pe_test(preflight=True) path: on failure, call classify_and_heal → apply edits → separate precursor commit 'chore: preflight auto-heal <recognizer>' → re-run pre-flight once → proceed or escalate.

Out of scope for MVP: additional recognizers (stale fixtures, import errors, timeout-too-short) — library grows per-ticket as patterns surface; auto-revert the heal commit if main ticket fails downstream; classifier confidence scoring.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/preflight_heal.py (new — module + Recognizer ABC + SocketRecvNoMockRecognizer + classify_and_heal), wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (wire into pe_test preflight-failure path, near lines ~1870), tests/test_preflight_heal.py (new).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (Recognizer docstring describes pattern→remedy contract), test-plan-or-why-not, oop-first (Recognizer class hierarchy, HealResult dataclass).
**Scope boundary:** IN — classify_and_heal framework, one SocketRecvNoMock recognizer, pe_chain wire-in, precursor-commit shape, re-run-once logic. OUT — additional recognizers, cross-ticket heal tracking, auto-revert, LLM-guided recognizer dispatch, repair of tests with FLAKY status semantics (that's T-hanging-tests-surfaced-by-timeout).
**Test plan:** (a) SocketRecvNoMockRecognizer.matches() returns True for fixture pytest output containing 'socket.timeout: ReadTimeout' and 'self._sock.recv'; (b) remedy() produces EditDict adding skipif decorator with correct indentation above the identified test function; (c) classify_and_heal() on matching fixture → HealResult.healed=True with one edit; (d) on non-matching → HealResult.healed=False and failure in unfixable; (e) integration: pe_test(preflight=True) on broken tree with the matching pattern, assert heal commit lands + pre-flight re-run is green + chain proceeds.
